checklist.label: Verification passed
checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN

checklist.label: Work committed
checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch

checklist.label: Implements the approved plan
checklist.detail: The doc covers stability contract, deprecation policy (announcement, grace period, sunset), and changelog expectation. However, the acceptance criteria explicitly requires documenting that 'renames go through a deprecation window with both old and new IDs served concurrently'. The doc separately notes that adding new providers is non-breaking and that deprecated IDs continue to work during the grace period, but it never explicitly states that during a rename both old and new IDs are served concurrently. An integrator must infer this from the combination of the two separate statements rather than seeing it called out directly.

checklist.label: Minimal and scoped
checklist.detail: Only 3 documentation files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new), README.md (one bullet link added), docs/API.md ('See also' section added). No source code touched, no unrelated churn.

checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking
checklist.detail: The new PROVIDER_IDS.md is 55 lines of substantive, real documentation covering the full provider table, stability contract, deprecation policy, and integrator expectations. No tests were altered or removed. No placeholder content.

checklist.label: Safe to advance
checklist.detail: The documentation is accurate: it lists 25 real providers with correct IDs, the stability guarantees are reasonable and match project norms, the deprecation policy is well-structured, and no security or correctness risks are introduced. Safe to advance to PR.

checklist.label: Judge confidence
checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar

dimensions.evidence: The doc covers stability contract, deprecation policy (announcement, grace period, sunset), and changelog expectation. However, the acceptance criteria explicitly requires documenting that 'renames go through a deprecation window with both old and new IDs served concurrently'. The doc separately notes that adding new providers is non-breaking and that deprecated IDs continue to work during the grace period, but it never explicitly states that during a rename both old and new IDs are served concurrently. An integrator must infer this from the combination of the two separate statements rather than seeing it called out directly.
Only 3 documentation files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new), README.md (one bullet link added), docs/API.md ('See also' section added). No source code touched, no unrelated churn.

The new PROVIDER_IDS.md is 55 lines of substantive, real documentation covering the full provider table, stability contract, deprecation policy, and integrator expectations. No tests were altered or removed. No placeholder content.

The documentation is accurate: it lists 25 real providers with correct IDs, the stability guarantees are reasonable and match project norms, the deprecation policy is well-structured, and no security or correctness risks are introduced. Safe to advance to PR.
